question: Can you find me the NYC elementary school in District 2 that ranked in the top 3 by performance category score over 3 school years starting from the school year with the highest suicide death rate between 2005 to 2010. For the neighborhood this elementary school is located in, who is it named after? Only report the year from 2005-2010 that has the highest suicide death rate for all persons. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Filtering to STUB_NAME = "Total" and STUB_LABEL = "All persons", the highest average suicide death rate in 2005-2010 occurs in 2010 at 12.25.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2005-2010 has the highest suicide death rate for all persons? (Filter STUB_NAME == "Total" and STUB_LABEL == "All persons"; average ESTIMATE by YEAR; select the maximum.)

2.fact: In 2010-11, the top 3 District 2 elementary schools by performance category score were: PS 77 Lower Lab School (18.8), PS 158 Bayard Taylor (16.0), and PS 290 Manhattan New School (14.9).
2.subquestion: Which NYC elementary schools in District 2 ranked in the top 3 by performance category score in the school year starting from <node_1 answer>?

3.fact: In 2011-12, the top 3 District 2 elementary schools by performance category score were: PS 77 Lower Lab School (21.0), PS 212 Midtown West (20.5), and PS 042 Benjamin Altman (20.2).
3.subquestion: Which NYC elementary schools in District 2 ranked in the top 3 by performance category score in 2011-12?

4.fact: In 2012-13, the top 3 District 2 elementary schools by performance category score were: PS 77 Lower Lab School (24.9), PS 124 Yung Wing (22.6), and PS 042 Benjamin Altman (21.2).
4.subquestion: Which NYC elementary schools in District 2 ranked in the top 3 by performance category score in 2012-13?

5.fact: PS 77 Lower Lab School is located at 1700 3rd Avenue in the Upper East Side-Carnegie Hill neighborhood of Manhattan.
5.subquestion: In which Manhattan neighborhood is <intersection of node_2, node_3, node_4: PS 77 Lower Lab School> located?

6.fact: The neighborhood is named after the mansion that Andrew Carnegie built in 1901 at Fifth Avenue and 91st Street.

7.fact: Andrew Carnegie (November 25, 1835 - August 11, 1919) was a Scottish-American industrialist and philanthropist.
7.subquestion: In what year was <node_6 answer> born?
